ops.body:
Closed in cookiesync v0.10.4 (origin/main 064494d, tag v0.10.4, Release workflow green, cask published to tap at 0.10.4). C1: readers (auth_status Get/Degraded, Wrap/Unwrap) now load the cached wrapper through an atomic.Pointer[SecureEnclaveWrapper] and never take the heal mutex, so an in-flight heal holding w.mu across the cache-newkey Touch ID subprocess no longer stalls status reads (TestDegradedReadsDoNotBlockOnAnInFlightHeal). C2: no consent-path change — heal/Close stay serialized under w.mu (one prompt at a time), whoami/request_consent/peerIsLive untouched.

Took five adversarial-review rounds (gpt-5.6-sol). KEY LESSON: moving cache-newkey OUT of the lock (singleflight, then a channel gate, then a healFlight) was the WRONG approach — each introduced dedup/shutdown/ctx races. The correct minimal fix keeps the original lock-across-subprocess heal/Close and only makes READERS lock-free via the atomic pointer. Close drops the Enclave key only after a successful cache-dropkey, so a failed drop stays retryable.
